calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Set For Case Management Conference On Nov-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Karen Sious Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joel Crane, Esq. 415-697-2000 of AGHW for Defendant KAREN SIOU.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KAREN SIOU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Karen Siou's summary judgment motion is granted.

Plaintiff alleges that defendant orally agreed to "[c]ollaborate on producing a written psychological diagnosis" and breached the agreement by not doing so. I grant summary judgment first because plaintiff failed to file a separate statement as required. (CCP 437c(b)(3).) Moreover, plaintiff states that defendant "kept wavering back and forth" and "at no time did we have an explicit agreement." (Def. Ex. F 25-26, 32, 33.) Thus, the alleged agreement was not definite enough to be enforced. (Ladas v. California State Automobile Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 770.) Plaintiff's inadmissible and irrelevant "evidence" creates no triable issue as to existence of an oral agreement.

I deny plaintiff's belated request to amend the complaint. Plaintiff noticed no such a motion, the request is untimely and prejudicial given next month's trial date, and the request appears to be a sham given plaintiff's discovery admissions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
